summary: OpenAgent is an open-source, cross-platform agentic AI IDE (desktop + web) with full MCP/Skills/Marketplace support and provider-agnostic adapters compatible with Codex and Claude ecosystems. This JSON is an execution-ready master prompt that guides scaffolding through production, CI/CD, QA, security hardening, and launch. It instructs agents and engineering teams to create a main branch, add the canonical repo, iterate via microtasks and CI/CD cycles, and continue until the product is production-ready, bug-free, and threat-free, with a final SWOT analysis.

repository.scaffold_branch_policy: create main branch, push initial scaffold, protect main with branch rules (require PR, CI passing)

goals: Deliver a production-ready cross-platform desktop (Electron) and web (React) IDE with feature parity
Implement agentic workflows: repo indexing, multi-file planning, staged diffs, one-shot and multi-shot agents
Support open provider adapters (OpenAI/Codex, Anthropic/Claude, Qwen, local runtimes)
Deliver MCP/Skills SDK, connector ecosystem, and an open marketplace with signed manifests
Ensure secure sandboxed execution, auditability, and enterprise policy controls
Ship CI/CD pipelines, automated tests, and release packaging for Linux/macOS/Windows
Achieve UI/UX quality goals: zero critical bugs, usability-tested flows, and threat-free UX

start_instructions.description: Create repository scaffold and initial project layout, then push to canonical repo and create protected main branch.
actions: Clone canonical repo URL locally or fork if needed
Add LICENSE (MIT or chosen OSS license), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and SECURITY.md
Add initial README.md with project vision and contribution guide
Commit scaffold and push to remote main branch
Enable branch protection: require PR reviews, require CI passing, require signed commits optional

start_instructions.description: Create project board and initial milestone structure for scaffold-to-production lifecycle.
actions: Create milestones: Discovery, MVP Scaffold, Core Features, Provider Adapters, MCP & Skills, Marketplace, Security Audit, Polish & Launch
Seed microtasks from masterplan into issue tracker

cycle_steps: open PR with checklist (tests, lint, security scan, AGENTS.md update if applicable)
CI runs (unit, integration with mocked adapters, sandbox security tests)
merge to main when CI and reviews pass
release candidate build and smoke tests
promote to production release when acceptance criteria met

automation.ci: GitHub Actions / GitLab CI templates included; use containerized runners with mocked adapters for tests
automation.cd: Automated packaging for Electron (Linux/macOS/Windows) and web deploy to static host; optional enterprise installers
automation.artifact_storage: S3-compatible or GitHub Releases for binaries and artifacts

microtasks.title: Microtask manifest schema
microtasks.description: Design JSON schema for microtasks including inputs, allowed actions, timeouts, approval policy, mcp_calls, and security constraints.

microtasks.title: OpenAgent CLI scaffold
microtasks.description: Implement CLI that validates manifests, snapshots repo index, plans steps, runs sandboxed execution, produces staged diffs and PR metadata.

microtasks.title: Repo indexer
microtasks.description: Index repository files, produce AST snippets, tokenized search index, and file-level metadata for planner context.

microtasks.title: Planner and staged diff generator
microtasks.description: Planner that converts goals into ordered edits and produces a staged diff patch that can be reviewed and applied.

microtasks.title: Sandboxed executor
microtasks.description: Containerized runner with strict FS and network policies, resource caps, and action whitelists.

microtasks.title: Provider adapter SDK and example adapters
microtasks.description: Adapter contract and example adapters for OpenAI, Anthropic, Qwen, and a local adapter for Ollama/LlamaCPP.

microtasks.title: MCP gateway and connector SDKs
microtasks.description: Gateway to route tool calls and connector SDKs for GitHub, Slack, CI, and Figma.

microtasks.title: Skills SDK and registry
microtasks.description: Runtime for skills, manifest signing, versioning, and discovery.

microtasks.title: Marketplace registry and review automation
microtasks.description: Open registry, automated tests for skill submissions, sandboxed review runner, and human review queue.

microtasks.title: Desktop UI skeleton and workspace UX
microtasks.description: Electron app with workspace explorer, editor integration, agent control panel, and staged diff UI.

microtasks.title: PR automation and CI templates
microtasks.description: Automated PR generation, changelog creation, test artifacts upload, and CI templates with mocked adapters.

microtasks.title: Docs, AGENTS.md examples, and onboarding
microtasks.description: Developer docs, AGENTS.md library of manifests, runbooks, and community contribution guide.

microtasks.title: Accessibility and UX polish
microtasks.description: Accessibility audit, keyboard navigation, color contrast, and usability testing sessions with fixes.

microtasks.title: Security hardening and audit fixes
microtasks.description: Run security scans, fix findings, perform penetration tests on sandbox and marketplace flows.

microtasks.title: Release packaging and installers
microtasks.description: Create Electron installers for Linux/macOS/Windows, web deploy pipeline, and release notes automation.

ci_pipeline.mocking_policy: Use mocked provider adapters in CI; no external API calls unless explicitly allowed and recorded
ci_pipeline.artifact_policy: Store test artifacts, staged diffs, and logs in artifact store; attach to PRs

cd_pipeline.web_deploy: Static hosting with CDN and versioned releases

quality_gates: No critical or high security findings

testing_matrix.integration_tests: end-to-end flows for agent harness, adapters, and marketplace
testing_matrix.e2e_ui_tests: desktop and web flows using Playwright or equivalent
testing_matrix.security_tests: SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, sandbox egress tests
testing_matrix.performance_tests: startup time, indexing throughput, memory caps under load

bug_management.triage_policy: Critical and high severity must be fixed before merge to main for release branches
bug_management.regression_policy: All regressions must have test coverage added

sandbox_controls.network: deny-by-default; allowlist endpoints for provider adapters and marketplace
sandbox_controls.filesystem: workspace-only + temp; no host root access

security_and_compliance.audit_and_logging: Immutable logs for agent decisions, tool calls, and user approvals; tamper-evident storage
security_and_compliance.privacy: Telemetry opt-in only; PII handling policy; data retention and deletion procedures documented in SECURITY.md

release_checks: Security audit passed and remediation completed
post_launch_support: Hotfix policy and SLA for critical bugs
Community support channels and issue templates

strengths: Open-source, provider-agnostic architecture enabling BYO models
Modular adapter and skills SDK for extensibility
Strong security posture with sandboxing and audit logs
weaknesses: High engineering complexity across adapters, sandboxing, and marketplace
Initial resource cost for secure sandbox and CI infrastructure
Dependency on third-party provider APIs for some features
opportunities: Growing demand for agentic developer tooling and automation
Community contributions to adapters and skills marketplace
Enterprise adoption for secure, auditable agent workflows
threats: Rapid provider API changes requiring adapter maintenance
Security vulnerabilities in sandbox or third-party connectors
Competition from established commercial products with deep pockets

for_weaknesses: Phase work into scaffold-to-MVP to reduce initial scope
Use mocked adapters in CI to reduce external dependency during development
for_threats: Maintain adapter abstraction layer to isolate provider changes
Continuous security testing and third-party audits
Active community governance and contribution guidelines

mvp: One-shot agent can run a manifest, produce staged diff, run tests, and generate PR metadata
Electron and web skeletons open workspace and show staged diffs
Provider adapters can be swapped without planner changes
Sandbox prevents network/FS egress in CI tests
production_ready: Marketplace accepts signed skill manifests and runs automated review
Security audit passed and remediation completed
UI/UX acceptance: no critical bugs, usability score >= target, WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
Release artifacts built, signed, and published

notes: Follow the lifecycle policy strictly: scaffold -> microtask -> CI/CD -> review -> merge -> release. Continue iterating microtasks until all milestones and acceptance criteria are met. Use the SWOT findings to prioritize security and adapter resilience work. This JSON is intended to be consumed by engineering teams or autonomous agents to orchestrate the full build-to-production lifecycle for OpenAgent.
